original: Tony Hawkis the first skateboarder to land a "900" (a 2½-revolution aerial spin) at the X Games in San Francisco, California
Complex carbon-based molecules found by Cassini spacecraft on Saturn moon Enceladus, previously only ever found on earth and meteorites in research published in "Nature"
Legendary video game and home computer Atari, Inc. founded byNolan Bushnelland Ted Dabney in Sunnyvale, California
Jim Henson's fantasy film "Labyrinth" written by Terry Jones, and starringDavid BowieandJennifer Connelly, is released in the US
Archaeologists in Roman city of Pompeii uncover a "pizza" painting in a fresco from house next to a bakery (more likely flatbread as lacks essential ingredients tomatoes and mozzarella)
World's first ATM is installed by Barclays Bank in Enfield, London
4th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Columbia 4 (STS-4) launches
American Annie Londonderry [Annie Kopchovsky] sets out from Boston to become first woman to bicycle around the world (completes journey September 1895)
Canadian-American adventurer Joshua Slocum arrives in Newport, Rhode Island, completing the 1st solo circumnavigation of the globe
Japanese spacecraft Hayabusa 2 arrives at diamond-shaped asteroid Ryugu
First rocket launch in Australia for 25 years and NASA's first commercial spaceport outside the US for sub-orbital rocket from Arnhem Space Centre, Northern Territory
Locust invasion labelled 'Swarmageddon' by The Times of India as it reaches Delhi
Flora MacDonald helpsBonnie Prince Charlie, disguised as Betty Burke an Irish maid, evade capture by landing him on the Isle of Skye
1st female state police graduates (NJ)
Capt. Lowell H. Smith and Lt. John P. Richter perform the first-ever aerial refueling in a DH-4B biplane
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 6,606 kph, 37,700 m
Heat dome envelops the Pacific Northwest with Portland posting highest temperature since records began of 112 F (broken the next day)
Hottest temperature ever recorded in Canada at 46.6 C (116 F) in Lytton, British Columbia (breaks record 2 days later with 49.6 C)
100°F (38°C), Fort Yukon, Alaska (state record)
118°F (47.8°C) at Lakewood, New Mexico a state record
Space shuttle STS-71 (Atlantis 14), launches
NASA launches space vehicle S-205
US Seasat 1, 1st oceanographic satellite, launched into polar orbit
Soyuz 30 carries 2 cosmonauts (1 Polish) to Salyut 6 space station
Soyuz T-9 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 7 space station
USAF Major Robert A Rushworth, flying in X-15 rocket powered aircraft, reaches altitude 285,000 feet (86,868 meters)
American tennis player Anne White shocks Wimbledon by wearing a white, one-piece, lycra body suit in 1st round match against Pam Shriver; wears regular outfit after rain break
MLB Cleveland Indians trailing Baltimore Orioles 9-1, come back to win 12-11 in 11 innings
Dodgers' Jerry Reuss no-hits SF Giants 8-0
Henry Rono of Kenya sets record for 3,000 m, 7:32.1
MLB Philadelphia Phillies Johnny Callison hits for cycle, but teammate centerfielder Tony Gonzalez's error ends his record 205 consecutive errorless games
MLB Seattle Mariners bat out of order against Chicago White Sox in 2nd inning

Denver Pop Festival opens; 50,000 attend;Frank Zappa,Creedence Clearwater Revival, andJimi Hendrixheadline; other performers include Three Dog Night, Tim Buckley, Big Mama Thornton, Johnny Winter,Joe Cocker, and Poco

Festival Express opens in Toronto, Canada - The Grateful Dead, The Band,Janis Joplin, Flying Burrito Bros, Buddy Guy, Great Speckled Bird, and Delaney & Bonnie & Friends cross Canada together by train, for 5 shows in 3 cities
New Amsterdam (now New York City) enacts first speed limit law in North America
First color television demonstration is performed by Bell Laboratories in NYC
"Captain Video & His Video Rangers" debut on DUMONT-TV - Television's first sci-fi series
Chlorophyll "A" synthesized in Cambridge, Massachusetts
The British Medical Research Council publishes a report suggesting a direct link between smoking and lung cancer.
First nuclear power station opens in Obninsk near Moscow, Russia
Canadian boxer George Dixon becomes the first Black world champion when he stops English bantamweight champion Edwin "Nunc" Wallace in 18 rounds in London
Russian sailors mutiny aboard the battleship "Potemkin" and sail for Odessa
FBI captures 8 Nazi saboteurs from a sub off NY's Long Island
New York and Boston are linked by telegraph wires
Newberry Medal 1st presented for kids literature (Hendrik Van Loon)
The Tower of Hercules in northern Spain - only preserved Roman lighthouse still in use is inscribed as a World Heritage Site
Juan Rodríguez Cabrillosets sail from the Mexican port of Navidad on the first European voyage to explore the west coast of North America for the Spanish Empire
Defending championJack Johnsonbeats fellow American Frank Moran on points in 20 rounds in Paris, France to retain his lineal heavyweight boxing title
Liberty Bell returns home to Philadelphia after the British departure
98°F (36.8°C) in De Bilt, Netherlands
Fanny Blankers-Koen runs Dutch record 200m (24.5)
1st NLer to get 6 hits in 9 inn game (Dave Force, Philadelphia Athletics)
Bill Graham's New York rock venue Fillmore East closes down, to be succeeded by Fillmore West in San Francisco
American TV variety program "The Flip Wilson Show" last airs on NBC-TV, after 4 seasons, winning 2 Emmy Awards
Billy J Kramer & Dakotas record Lennon-McCartney song "I Call Your Name"
"Sie Liebt Dich (She Loves You)" by The Beatles, singing in German, peaks at #97
Dutch soccer clubs Rapid JC and Roda Sports merge to form Roda JC Kerkrade; KNVB Cup winners 1996–97, 1999–2000
Honduras/El Salvador breaks diplomatic relations due to soccer match
Prudence Crandall, a white woman, arrested for conducting an academy for black females at Canterbury, Connecticut
Djibouti (Afars & Issas) claims Independence from France
